variants.label: Standard Canadian Patent Application
variants.description: Canadian patent application under the Patent Act and Patent Rules, including direct filings and PCT national phase entries.

ui.helpText: Short technical designation. Avoid trademarks and non-technical wording.

ui.placeholder: List related CA, foreign or PCT applications.
ui.helpText: Used for priority/benefit claims; supply application numbers and dates.
1–3 sentences indicating the technical field, consistent with abstract rules.

ui.placeholder: Describe prior art known to the applicant.
ui.helpText: Summarize relevant prior art and its shortcomings without admitting obviousness.

ui.placeholder: Provide the gist of the invention.
ui.helpText: Explain the problem, the solution, and essential features as required by Canadian disclosure rules.
One line per figure; required only when drawings are included.

sections.label: Mode(s) for Carrying Out the Invention

ui.placeholder: Provide sufficient detail to enable a skilled person.
ui.helpText: Canadian Patent Act s.27(3): Must correctly and fully describe at least one workable mode. Preferred embodiments encouraged.
Claims must be clear, concise and fully supported by the description as required by the Patent Rules.
≤150 words; technical field, problem, solution and use. No advantages or value statements (per CIPO abstract guidelines).

sequenceListing.lateFurnishingNotes: ST.26 sequence listing may be furnished after filing or at national phase entry without loss of filing date.

title.message: Title appears long; consider making it more concise.

claims.message: More than 20 claims may increase examination fees.

crossSectionChecks.message: Some claims may lack explicit support in the detailed description.
Abstract may cover features not reflected in the independent claims.

title.instruction: Draft a concise technical title suitable for a Canadian patent application.

topUp.instruction: Per CIPO guidelines and Patent Rules, draft a title that is brief, technically descriptive, and identifies the invention.
constraints: No trade names, trade marks, or personal names
Suitable for both English and French examination
additions: Canada is bilingual - title should be clear in both languages

constraints: Specify the technical field without describing embodiments or advantages.

topUp.instruction: Per CIPO practice, draft Technical Field indicating the area to which the invention pertains.
Per CIPO practice, draft Technical Field indicating the technical area of the invention.

constraints: Summarize prior art known to the applicant and key limitations.
Avoid explicit admissions that cited references are prior art unless intentional.

topUp.instruction: Per CIPO guidelines, draft Background Art describing relevant prior technology without prejudicing patentability.
constraints: Summarize relevant prior art and limitations
additions: Canada has duty of candour but no formal IDS like USPTO
Do not deliberately conceal material prior art

summary.instruction: Draft the Disclosure of Invention section.
constraints: State the technical problem and the inventive solution.
Ensure consistency with the independent claims.

topUp.instruction: Per Canadian Patent Rules, draft Disclosure of Invention explaining the problem, solution, and essential features.
additions: Disclosure should meet s.27(3) of the Patent Act requirements

topUp.instruction: Per CIPO formatting guidelines, briefly describe each drawing figure.

modes_for_carrying_out.instruction: Draft the Mode(s) for Carrying Out the Invention.
constraints: Describe embodiments with enough detail for a skilled person to work the invention (s.27(3)).
Ensure each independent claim has at least one supporting embodiment.

topUp.instruction: Per s.27(3) of the Canadian Patent Act, draft Mode(s) for Carrying Out the Invention correctly and fully describing the invention.
constraints: At least one workable mode must be described
Sufficient detail for skilled person to work invention
Each independent claim needs supporting embodiment
additions: Canada does NOT require best mode disclosure (unlike US)
But must correctly and fully describe at least one workable mode
Preferred embodiments encouraged but not mandatory

topUp.instruction: Per s.27(3) of the Canadian Patent Act, draft detailed description correctly and fully describing the invention.
constraints: Sufficient for skilled person to practice
additions: No best mode requirement in Canada

claims.instruction: Draft a full set of claims compliant with Canadian practice.
constraints: Use 'comprising' unless a closed transition is required.
Claims must be clear, concise and supported by the description.

topUp.instruction: Per Patent Rules and CIPO Manual of Patent Office Practice (MOPOP), draft claims that are clear, concise, and supported.
constraints: Multiple dependent claims allowed (including on other multiple dependents)
additions: No per-claim fees for excess claims in Canada
Unity assessed under 'single general inventive concept' standard

constraints: State the technical field, problem, solution and principal use.
Do not include advantages, value statements or claim language.

topUp.instruction: Per CIPO abstract guidelines, draft an Abstract summarizing the technical disclosure for searching purposes.
constraints: Technical field, problem, solution, principal use
No advantages, value statements, or claim language
additions: Abstract is for information/search purposes only
Reference illustrative figure if drawings present

sectionHeadings.modes_for_carrying_out: MODE(S) FOR CARRYING OUT THE INVENTION

diagramGenerationHints.block: Use rectangles and arrows for components and flow; match numerals with description.
diagramGenerationHints.flowchart: Use standard symbols for method steps; keep consistent with claimed method flow.

checkList.description: Verify that all reference numerals in drawings are defined in the description.
Verify that major claimed elements appear in at least one figure when drawings are provided.
